home.title: Alejandro Ortiz Corro
home.summary: Product engineer building websites, dashboards, workflows, and private business tools that help teams sell, operate, and ship with less friction.
home.seoDescription: Alejandro Ortiz Corro is a product engineer building reliable business software: public websites, private dashboards, backend workflows, payments, lead flows, AI-assisted delivery practices, and release systems for teams that need quality and scale.

homeContent.headline: Software that moves real work.
homeContent.deck: I design and build sites, dashboards, and workflows your team can operate, review, and release with confidence.
homeContent.proofBoardBody: Public proof stays visible. Client code, operational data, and unfinished product details stay protected.

homeContent.architectureHeading: System boundaries your team can rely on.
homeContent.architectureBody: Public pages stay fast. Private dashboards stay protected. Contact, analytics, previews, and production releases each have an explicit boundary.

architectureStages.label: Public surface
architectureStages.text: Public pages use stable IDs, localized slugs, canonical URLs, and sitemap rules.

architectureStages.label: Private surface
architectureStages.text: Dashboard routes stay authenticated, noindex, and separate from what search engines should see.

architectureStages.label: Release path
architectureStages.text: Changes go through preview first; production promotion is separate and reviewable.

homeContent.practiceHeading: Release paths stay visible from idea to production.
homeContent.practiceBody: I start with the business pressure, then make the software dependable where it matters: clear data, safe auth, visible failures, and releases people can trust.

practicePoints.label: Clarify the work
practicePoints.text: Turn loose goals into flows, constraints, boundaries, and a path to release.

practicePoints.label: Build the system
practicePoints.text: Make UI, data, auth, communication, observability, and deployment fit together.

practicePoints.label: Prove it early
practicePoints.text: Use tests, browser QA, protected branches, preview checks, and production verification so problems show up before customers do.

homeContent.contactHeading: What does your team need to stop improvising?
homeContent.contactBody: Bring the operational pressure, broken workflow, or release that needs a clearer path. We will turn it into the next right system and a practical way to ship it.

case-studies.title: Selected work
case-studies.summary: Live sites, active product builds, enterprise delivery context, and the engineering practice behind this public sample.
case-studies.seoDescription: Read public work notes by Alejandro Ortiz Corro across production websites, active product builds, private business systems, confidential enterprise delivery, and modern software engineering practice.

case-study:casa-roca.title: Casa Roca public site and conversion path
case-study:casa-roca.summary: A live hospitality site with a clear public story, direct contact path, and production delivery discipline.
case-study:casa-roca.seoDescription: Casa Roca is a live hospitality website for a boutique stay in Veracruz, with clear place storytelling, direct guest inquiry paths, search-ready pages, and production release discipline.

caseStudyContent.overview: Casa Roca is the public website for a boutique stay in Roca Partida, Veracruz. It helps guests understand the place quickly, decide whether it fits their trip, and contact the business without exposing reservations or back-office work.

problem.title: Problem
problem.body: The business needed a public presence that could communicate place, experience, and credibility without exposing reservation details, internal media work, or private administration.

businessOutcome.title: Business outcome
businessOutcome.body: The production site gives Casa Roca a public destination for guests, search traffic, brand trust, and direct inquiries while keeping the back office separate.

role.title: Role
role.body: Public site delivery, bilingual surface planning, SEO metadata, release verification, performance care, and a clear boundary between public pages and private operational workflows.

constraints.title: Constraints
constraints.body: The case study can show the live public website and architecture decisions, but it must avoid private booking data, admin dashboard details, credentials, internal code, and operational records.

architectureDecisions.title: Architecture decisions
architectureDecisions.body: Keep the guest-facing pages fast and easy to inspect, treat bilingual content and SEO metadata as first-class concerns, and keep private booking and administration workflows outside the public site.

executionHighlights.title: Execution highlights
executionHighlights.body: The work emphasized clear hospitality positioning, localized routes, production deployment discipline, content safety, and a live website that anyone can open without privileged access.

qualitySecurityPerformance.title: Quality, security, and performance
qualitySecurityPerformance.body: The public surface is live, avoids sensitive operational data, uses explicit metadata, and is framed around fast, stable, search-readable pages.

publicEvidence.label: Production website
publicEvidence.description: Open the live Casa Roca website.

confidentialityNote.title: Confidentiality note
confidentialityNote.body: Only the public website and public-safe delivery context are shown here. Private client code, booking records, admin workflows, credentials, analytics, and operational data remain private.

case-study:the-barber-central.title: The Barber Central booking and operations system
case-study:the-barber-central.summary: An active booking and operations product shaped around the daily workflow of service businesses.
case-study:the-barber-central.seoDescription: The Barber Central shows active product architecture for booking, operations, payment readiness, and customer communication workflows.

caseStudyContent.overview: The Barber Central is an active product build for service businesses that need booking, availability, customer communication, and payments to feel like one system instead of a pile of tools.

problem.title: Problem
problem.body: Local service businesses often run bookings, reminders, staff operations, and payments across disconnected tools, which makes growth harder and creates avoidable manual work.

businessOutcome.title: Business outcome
businessOutcome.body: The product direction centers the daily operating workflow: book clients, manage availability, communicate clearly, and keep the business moving without stitching tools together by hand.

role.title: Role
role.body: Product architecture, workflow modeling, full-stack implementation planning, booking flow decisions, payments readiness, customer communication, and quality gates for an active build.

constraints.title: Constraints
constraints.body: The build is still in development, so the public page must avoid private business data, unfinished dashboard internals, secrets, and customer records while still showing the product judgment.

architectureDecisions.title: Architecture decisions
architectureDecisions.body: Keep the public page sharp, make the operational dashboard task-first, and treat provider integrations as explicit business boundaries instead of hidden glue.

executionHighlights.title: Execution highlights
executionHighlights.body: The work covers booking IA, operations dashboard shape, provider setup, environment discipline, and manual browser QA for flows where auth, origin, and preview behavior matter.

qualitySecurityPerformance.title: Quality, security, and performance
qualitySecurityPerformance.body: The project emphasizes protected environments, explicit verification commands, browser QA, source checks, and careful handling of auth/provider configuration before public launch.

publicEvidence.label: Development preview
publicEvidence.description: Open the development landing page for The Barber Central.
publicEvidence.altText: The Barber Central development landing page

confidentialityNote.title: Confidentiality note
confidentialityNote.body: Only the product direction and public development page are shown. Private implementation details, secrets, test data, customer records, provider dashboards, and unpublished business information stay private.

case-study:nutri-plan.title: Nutri Plan private workflow system
case-study:nutri-plan.summary: A private product system for client workflows, plans, dashboards, and educational content.
case-study:nutri-plan.seoDescription: Nutri Plan shows active product work around client workflows, structured plans, operational dashboards, educational content, and delivery quality.

caseStudyContent.overview: Nutri Plan is a private product system for nutrition workflows, dashboards, mobile access, educational content, and operational clarity across several surfaces.

problem.title: Problem
problem.body: Nutrition operations need connected client records, plans, dashboards, and mobile experiences, but the domain becomes fragile when workflow state is split across unrelated tools.

businessOutcome.title: Business outcome
businessOutcome.body: The build organizes the product around real operational roles and shared backend state so client work, admin work, and mobile access can come from one model.

role.title: Role
role.body: Product workflow architecture, dashboard planning, backend state modeling, mobile surface coordination, quality practice, and release discipline for a private active build.

constraints.title: Constraints
constraints.body: The product is private, so the public case study can discuss architecture and outcomes only at a safe level without exposing patient-like records, business data, private code, or internal workflows.

architectureDecisions.title: Architecture decisions
architectureDecisions.body: Keep the backend as a shared source of truth, separate public landing concerns from private dashboard/mobile workflows, and make environment and QA gates part of release readiness.

executionHighlights.title: Execution highlights
executionHighlights.body: The work spans landing, dashboard, admin dashboard, mobile app, Convex-backed state, route QA, preview verification, and operational parity with the reference system.

qualitySecurityPerformance.title: Quality, security, and performance
qualitySecurityPerformance.body: The project uses functional QA, route sweeps, environment separation, protected release branches, and preview validation when auth, routing, UI, or behavior changes.

publicEvidence.label: Sanitized preview
publicEvidence.description: Review the sanitized product screen and workflow notes for a private active build.

confidentialityNote.title: Confidentiality note
confidentialityNote.body: This case does not publish private product code, client records, health-related data, credentials, private dashboard screens, or operational provider details.

case-study:enterprise-systems.title: Enterprise systems under confidential constraints
case-study:enterprise-systems.summary: Confidential enterprise work summarized through outcomes, architecture judgment, escalation leadership, and release support.
case-study:enterprise-systems.seoDescription: Enterprise Systems summarizes confidential software delivery through business outcomes, architecture judgment, escalation leadership, backend delivery, and quality practice.

caseStudyContent.overview: Enterprise work cannot be shown as screenshots or source code. What can be shown is the kind of responsibility involved: escalations, payment plans, cashback, customs workflows, backend delivery, and reliability inside complex environments.

problem.title: Problem
problem.body: Large systems accumulate operational edge cases, integration pressure, and support escalations where the cost of unclear ownership is high.

businessOutcome.title: Business outcome
businessOutcome.body: The value is reliable delivery under constraints: untangling escalations, shipping backend workflows, and improving operational paths without exposing confidential employer or client material.

role.title: Role
role.body: Backend delivery, escalation leadership, payment-plan and cashback work, customs workflow implementation, cross-team coordination, and production-minded problem solving.

constraints.title: Constraints
constraints.body: Specific code, incidents, customer data, employer systems, and internal metrics are confidential. The case study must stay at the level of patterns, responsibilities, and outcomes.

architectureDecisions.title: Architecture decisions
architectureDecisions.body: Work from the business workflow backward, isolate risky integration points, keep operational contracts explicit, and favor maintainable backend paths over clever one-off fixes.

executionHighlights.title: Execution highlights
executionHighlights.body: Public-safe examples include payment plan workflows, cashback implementation, customs handling, escalation ownership, and backend delivery across complex organizational contexts.

qualitySecurityPerformance.title: Quality, security, and performance
qualitySecurityPerformance.body: The work depends on careful change management, production awareness, data sensitivity, clear handoffs, and testing around workflows where mistakes become operational risk.

publicEvidence.label: Confidential summary
publicEvidence.description: Read the enterprise delivery summary without private employer, customer, incident, or source-code details.

confidentialityNote.title: Confidentiality note
confidentialityNote.body: No private employer code, customer data, incidents, internal metrics, credentials, or proprietary architecture details are published in this public case study.

case-study:engineering-practice.title: Engineering practice for reviewable releases
case-study:engineering-practice.summary: Modern agent-assisted engineering framed through planning, QA, architecture, observability, deploys, and documentation.
case-study:engineering-practice.seoDescription: Engineering Practice explains Alejandro Ortiz Corro's modern agent-assisted software delivery process across planning, QA, architecture, observability, deployment, and documentation.

caseStudyContent.overview: Engineering Practice is the operating system behind the work: structured discovery, TDD, agent-assisted implementation, browser QA, release discipline, observability, and documentation.

problem.title: Problem
problem.body: Modern software work moves quickly, but speed without architecture, tests, review, and production checks produces fragile systems and weak evidence.

businessOutcome.title: Business outcome
businessOutcome.body: A repeatable engineering process lets projects move faster while still producing inspectable decisions, safer releases, better handoffs, and stronger confidence for business stakeholders.

role.title: Role
role.body: Technical planning, architecture review, TDD flow, agent orchestration, browser QA, release train discipline, source-quality checks, and documentation as part of delivery.

constraints.title: Constraints
constraints.body: The process should demonstrate modern AI-assisted work without pretending every private client project is public or reducing delivery quality to tool choice.

architectureDecisions.title: Architecture decisions
architectureDecisions.body: Use clear seams, graph-backed public content, explicit environment contracts, protected branches, focused tests, and manual browser verification for visible behavior.

executionHighlights.title: Execution highlights
executionHighlights.body: This public site itself is the process evidence: PR review, TDD tracers, Impeccable visual checks, GitHub issue flow, release documentation, and browser QA.

qualitySecurityPerformance.title: Quality, security, and performance
qualitySecurityPerformance.body: Quality includes source checks, type checks, behavior tests, build verification, visual QA, no private data in public routes, and deployment paths that can be audited.

publicEvidence.label: Process evidence
publicEvidence.description: Public source, tests, documentation, and PR history show the engineering workflow behind the site.
publicEvidence.altText: Public-safe process evidence for AOHYS engineering practice

confidentialityNote.title: Confidentiality note
confidentialityNote.body: The process is public where this repository is public. Private client code, private product code, credentials, screenshots, and operational data remain outside this sample.

practice.title: Delivery stays visible from brief to release.
practice.summary: Planning, QA, review loops, and release discipline shaped into a clear path from request to dependable software.
practice.seoDescription: AOHYS Practice shows modern AI-assisted engineering, test discipline, review loops, quality gates, and release habits.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.summary: The engineering decisions behind this site: public pages, private dashboard, contact flow, observability, and release checks.
architecture.seoDescription: Architecture notes for Alejandro Ortiz Corro's public source sample: Astro SEO pages, private dashboard boundaries, Cloudflare deploys, Convex workflows, PostHog observability, and preview-to-production release checks.

architectureContent.heading: Public code for systems that stay private.
architectureContent.deck: Client code stays private. This repo shows how I think about the parts around a product: content structure, auth boundaries, releases, errors, providers, and the dashboard surface.

sections.title: Public surface
sections.body: Astro owns the public pages. The route map comes from stable content IDs, localized slugs, canonical metadata, language alternates, sitemap eligibility, and robots behavior instead of scattered page constants.

sections.title: Private boundary
sections.body: The dashboard belongs on the same domain, but it is a different surface: authenticated, omitted from the sitemap, and noindex. The public page can show the boundary without making private work public.

sections.title: Release Train
sections.body: Feature branches target develop first. Production moves through a separate main promotion path with Cloudflare/Wrangler checks, environment validation, and smoke verification before the canonical domain changes.

sections.title: Environment Contract
sections.body: Local, preview, and production settings are treated as an explicit contract. Provider dashboards may help setup, but durable deploy-time values should reconcile back to GitHub Environments.

sections.title: Public Content Graph
sections.body: The graph is the seam between content and routes. It owns locale variants, public paths, SEO metadata, sitemap behavior, safe evidence labels, and future dashboard publishing invariants.

sections.title: Provider responsibilities
sections.body: Cloudflare handles hosting and media delivery, Convex owns application state and dashboard workflows, PostHog owns analytics and errors, and Resend handles lead notifications. Technology appears as evidence, not as the sales pitch.

architectureContent.boundaryNote: This repository is a public working sample. It does not make private client code, product code, credentials, private screenshots, or operational data public.

summary: Senior product engineer with 9+ years of experience across React, TypeScript, Next.js, product architecture, performance, SEO, and maintainable UI systems.
I have shipped across enterprise systems, startup products, healthcare workflows, e-commerce platforms, public business sites, and systems serving 30.5M+ monthly users.
My recent work goes beyond screens: Next.js as a backend surface, backend workflow delivery, escalation ownership, payment plans, cashback, customs workflows, and cross-functional product execution.
I work with agents, TDD, browser QA, release discipline, observability, and documentation so ambiguous goals become software teams can operate and improve.

highlights.label: 30.5M+ monthly users
highlights.text: Contributed to e-commerce platform work at Datazone/AutoZone with SEO, performance, React, and React Native modernization.

highlights.label: 80% load-time reduction
highlights.text: Executed major Next.js migration work at Tala Mobile, reducing load times from 3-5 seconds to under 1 second.

highlights.label: Mission-critical dispatch
highlights.text: Implemented keyboard dispatch workflows for a major US emergency response platform with zero-error tolerance requirements.

highlights.label: Public working sample
highlights.text: Built this site so technical evaluators can review SEO, i18n, content graph, QA, branch protection, and release practice.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.summary: How AOHYS treats contact data, analytics, errors, and private project information.
privacy.seoDescription: AOHYS privacy route explaining contact data, analytics, error reporting, private project boundaries, and public code limits.

privacyContent.heading: Privacy
privacyContent.intro: This site is public code as an engineering sample, but private client work and operational data stay private.

sections.title: Contact requests
sections.body: When you send the contact form, AOHYS uses your name, email, optional phone/company, preferred contact path, intent, and message only to respond to the request.

sections.title: Analytics and errors
sections.body: PostHog is used for explicit pageviews, selected CTA/form events, and browser error signals. Autocapture is disabled, and contact message text is not sent to analytics.

sections.title: Private project boundaries
sections.body: This repository is public for review. Private client code, credentials, operational records, and dashboard data are not public.

privacyContent.boundaryNote: The private dashboard under /dashboard is authenticated, noindexed, excluded from the sitemap, and separate from public SEO pages.
